Badger Grapple Red — v20.6 All Areas Repainted

Completes the environment art pass — all 7 painted area backgrounds now
use the same procedural shading pipeline as Field House/Campus Quad
(v20.4): multi-tone gradients, dithering, directional texture.

- area_lakeshore.png: a real lake (water shading with wave highlights)
  placed exactly on the actual blocked water-collision zone (px 272-448,
  32-112 — checked against isBlocked before painting, not guessed), tall
  grass matching the real isGrass zone, a small dock/sand edge.
- area_downtown.png: building facades (shop + apartment style, alternating)
  lining both sides of a paved street corridor with lane markings and
  sidewalk texture, matching downtown's actual "buildings top and bottom,
  walkway through the middle" collision layout exactly.
- area_river.png: a forest mass (layered tree-cluster shading) placed on
  the real blocked forest zone, a decorative river placed only in the
  guaranteed-always-blocked border row (so it can't create a visual/
  collision mismatch), matching tall grass zone.
- area_conference.png / area_championship.png: proper arena interiors —
  bleacher bands top and bottom, a larger shaded ring/mat, and venue
  banners ("CONFERENCE" / "CHAMPIONSHIP") — these are the first and
  final gym-badge battle locations, so they're built to feel like a
  bigger stage than Field House rather than reusing the same room.

Every element in every scene was checked against the actual isBlocked /
isGrass pixel math from src/data/maps.js before placement — same
discipline as the Field House mat fix last round, applied proactively
this time instead of caught after the fact.

VERIFIED: all 5 new files confirmed 448x224 before packaging. Reached
Lakeshore, River Trail, and Conference Arena by literally clicking
through the real navigation path in a live headless browser (Field
House -> door -> Campus Quad -> real exit tiles), not by placing files
and assuming — zero console errors on every screen.

Cache: ?v=206. Proof: title/note reads "v20.6 All Areas Repainted".
